name: Merrill Lynch Financial Advisor Jason Salgado
description_short: The Waldele Group is a Merrill Lynch Wealth Management team in New York specializing in wealth management and financial planning for high-net-worth families.
description_long:
The Waldele Group operates as part of Merrill Lynch Wealth Management, a division of Bank of America, headquartered at 225 Liberty Street in New York City. The team focuses exclusively on serving ultra-high-net-worth individuals and families who have achieved significant personal and professional success. As of February 2026, they oversee approximately $7 billion in assets for a select clientele across the United States.

The Waldele Group offers comprehensive wealth management services including personalized investment solutions, retirement and savings account planning, lending products through Bank of America, insurance solutions, and customized wealth planning strategies. They provide access to a broad range of investment products from Merrill Lynch combined with banking services from Bank of America. Their approach emphasizes understanding client priorities, designing customized strategies, implementing wealth management plans, and conducting periodic reviews to help clients pursue financial goals while managing investment risk.

The team distinguishes itself through multi-generational relationship-building, emphasis on customization and continuity of service, and a track record of national recognition. Members have been named to Barron's Top 100 Private Wealth Teams (2021), Forbes Top 250 Wealth Advisors in America (2016-2023), and Barron's Top 100 Financial Advisors in America (2011-2018). They explicitly position themselves as advisors to families managing significant wealth complexity rather than transaction-focused financial services providers.

This is a private wealth management firm designed exclusively for ultra-high-net-worth clients, not a consumer banking or lending service for general populations. Access and service levels are highly selective, and the firm does not publicly disclose minimum asset requirements or availability to retail customers.

pros: Manages approximately $7 billion in assets under advisement as of 2026
Team recognized by Barron's Top 100 Private Wealth Management Teams (2021)
Listed on Forbes Top 250 Wealth Advisors in America annually from 2016-2023
Multi-generational wealth management experience serving select families nationwide
Integrated access to both Merrill Lynch investment products and Bank of America banking services
Emphasis on customized wealth planning strategies rather than standardized products
Dedicated team approach providing continuity and relationship-focused service
cons: Exclusive clientele model means not accessible to average consumers or families with moderate assets
No published information about minimum asset requirements or service availability
Focused on wealth preservation for ultra-high-net-worth individuals, not wealth building for broader markets
Limited transparency about fees, specific investment performance, or service terms on public website
No indication of specialized consumer lending, credit products, or financial accessibility services
best_for: Ultra-high-net-worth families with $7M+ in investable assets seeking coordinated wealth management
Successful business owners and professionals requiring multi-generational wealth planning
Individuals needing integrated investment and banking services from a nationally recognized team
Families seeking customized strategies addressing complex tax, estate, and investment concerns
diagnosis: The Waldele Group is best suited for ultra-high-net-worth families ($7M+) seeking comprehensive, relationship-based wealth management from a nationally recognized Merrill Lynch team. The primary caveat is that this is an exclusive private wealth management practice—not a consumer banking service—and is not designed for or accessible to individuals or families with typical wealth levels or banking needs.
services: Personalized wealth management strategy design and implementation
Wealth planning and estate planning consultation
Personal banking products through Bank of America
Access to research and market insights through BullPen platform
Client relationship management and dedicated advisor access
